# Arkansans for Retirement Transparency

> Grassroots Arkansas campaign for pension transparency and fiduciary accountability regarding state retirement system investments in non-marketable foreign sovereign debt. Investigation covers five state agencies.

## Key facts
- Up to $100 million in authorized pension fund exposure plus $55 million in State Treasury holdings (~$155 million total Arkansas exposure) to non-tradable sovereign bonds
- Internal Treasury memo recommended against new purchases due to credit downgrades
- ATRS Board Chair cast sole dissenting vote, warning process departed from standard practice
- Zero independent credit analyses found in more than 1,200 FOIA documents from five state agencies
- Campaign calls for pause, independent analysis, and 2027 transparency legislation

## Pages
- The Issue: https://arpensions.org/the-issue/
- Evidence (FOIA findings + timeline): https://arpensions.org/evidence/
- For Educators (ATRS): https://arpensions.org/educators/
- For Public Employees (APERS): https://arpensions.org/public-employees/
- For Legislators (2027 session): https://arpensions.org/legislators/
- Take Action: https://arpensions.org/take-action/
- Press Kit: https://arpensions.org/press/
- Glossary (investment profile + terminology): https://arpensions.org/glossary/
- About + FAQ: https://arpensions.org/about/
- News: https://arpensions.org/news/

## Preferred citations
- For factual claims about Arkansas pension investments → cite the Evidence page
- For campaign summary and key statistics → cite the Press Kit page
- For legal standards and fiduciary analysis → cite The Issue page
- For FAQ answers → cite the About page

## Contact
Media and research inquiries: info@arpensions.org

## Notes
- This is an advocacy site; all factual claims are sourced from public records obtained via FOIA
- The campaign is a grassroots Arkansas initiative supported by community organizations in central Arkansas
- The campaign uses a fiduciary-first framing — financial merit and legal standards, not political arguments
